benchmark_honesty_caveat: Autonomous second-round repair-context selector output. Hidden aligned target Lean declarations, statements, names, and proofs are not included. Mathlib signatures here are included only after Lean #check hydration.

checked_signatures.name: Finset.sum_singleton
checked_signatures.signature:
Finset.sum_singleton.{u_1, u_4} {ι : Type u_1} {M : Type u_4} [AddCommMonoid M] (f : ι → M) (a : ι) :
  ∑ x ∈ {a}, f x = f a
checked_signatures.why_needed: to evaluate nipatWeightSum over a singleton set

checked_signatures.name: Ring.choose_natCast
checked_signatures.signature:
Ring.choose_natCast.{u_1} {R : Type u_1} [NonAssocRing R] [Pow R ℕ] [BinomialRing R] [NatPowAssoc R] (n k : ℕ) :
  Ring.choose (↑n) k = ↑(n.choose k)
checked_signatures.why_needed: connects Ring.choose to standard binomial coefficients

checked_signatures.name: Nat.choose
checked_signatures.why_needed: standard binomial coefficient for natural numbers

checked_signatures.name: coeff_mul
checked_signatures.signature:
PowerSeries.coeff_mul.{u_1} {R : Type u_1} [Semiring R] (n : ℕ) (φ ψ : R⟦X⟧) :
  (coeff n) (φ * ψ) = ∑ p ∈ antidiagonal n, (coeff p.1) φ * (coeff p.2) ψ
checked_signatures.why_needed: convolution formula for product of power series

checked_signatures.name: coeff_rescale
checked_signatures.signature:
PowerSeries.coeff_rescale.{u_1} {R : Type u_1} [CommSemiring R] (f : R⟦X⟧) (a : R) (n : ℕ) :
  (coeff n) ((rescale a) f) = a ^ n * (coeff n) f

checked_signatures.name: Finset.sum_pow
checked_signatures.signature:
Finset.sum_pow.{u_1, u_2} {α : Type u_1} {R : Type u_2} [DecidableEq α] [CommSemiring R] {s : Finset α} (x : α → R)
  (n : ℕ) : s.sum x ^ n = ∑ k ∈ s.sym n, ↑(↑k).multinomial * (Multiset.map x ↑k).prod
checked_signatures.why_needed: expansion of power of sum - need to understand actual return type

checked_signatures.name: Finset.sym
checked_signatures.signature: Finset.sym.{u_1} {α : Type u_1} [DecidableEq α] (s : Finset α) (n : ℕ) : Finset (Sym α n)
checked_signatures.why_needed: multiset of size n from finset s

checked_signatures.name: Sym
checked_signatures.signature: Sym.{u_1} (α : Type u_1) (n : ℕ) : Type u_1
checked_signatures.why_needed: understand the type returned by sum_pow

checked_signatures.name: Fintype.piFinset
checked_signatures.signature:
Fintype.piFinset.{u_1, u_4} {α : Type u_1} [DecidableEq α] [Fintype α] {δ : α → Type u_4} (t : (a : α) → Finset (δ a)) :
  Finset ((a : α) → δ a)
checked_signatures.why_needed: finite set of all functions into finite codomain

checked_signatures.name: Finset.sum_map
checked_signatures.signature:
Finset.sum_map.{u_1, u_2, u_3} {ι : Type u_1} {κ : Type u_2} {M : Type u_3} [AddCommMonoid M] (s : Finset ι) (e : ι ↪ κ)
  (f : κ → M) : ∑ x ∈ Finset.map e s, f x = ∑ x ∈ s, f (e x)
checked_signatures.why_needed: convert between different index types for sums

checked_signatures.name: MvPolynomial.esymm
checked_signatures.signature: MvPolynomial.esymm.{u_5, u_6} (σ : Type u_5) (R : Type u_6) [CommSemiring R] [Fintype σ] (n : ℕ) : MvPolynomial σ R
checked_signatures.why_needed: elementary symmetric function as multivariate polynomial

checked_signatures.name: MvPolynomial.esymm_eq_sum_subtype
checked_signatures.signature:
MvPolynomial.esymm_eq_sum_subtype.{u_5, u_6} (σ : Type u_5) (R : Type u_6) [CommSemiring R] [Fintype σ] (n : ℕ) :
  MvPolynomial.esymm σ R n = ∑ t, ∏ i ∈ ↑t, MvPolynomial.X i
checked_signatures.why_needed: explicit formula for elementary symmetric function

missing_or_uncertain_context.item: Definition of nipatWeightSum and its computation for singleton sets
Ring.choose properties for negative first argument
Exact statement of Finset.sum_pow and its return type
Whether Sym (Fin d) n is equivalent to Fin n → Fin d for the purpose of this sum
Fintype instance for Fin d → ZMod 2 or similar finite function spaces
Definition of esymmExt analogous to hsymmExt
Definition of skew Schur function s_{λ/μ}
Statement of first Jacobi-Trudi theorem jacobiTrudi_h
Any duality/omega involution relating h_n and e_n via transpose

proof_strategy_notes.failure_analysis: Cannot prove: missing lemmas that the only non-intersecting path tuple for the Dyck endpoints is identity-matched, and that each such path has weight 1.
proof_strategy_notes.proof_strategy_note: Use lgv_nonpermutable + catalanHankelMatrix_eq_pathWeightMatrix, then prove a same-unit helper that nipatWeightSum equals 1 by showing the non-intersecting set is a singleton of identity paths with unit weight.

proof_strategy_notes.failure_analysis: Cannot prove: missing binomial coefficient identities for negative upper arguments (Ring.choose (-n) k * (-1)^k = Ring.choose (n+k-1) k) and connection to (1-X)^{-n} coefficients.
proof_strategy_notes.proof_strategy_note: Use key_product_identity' which already proves (1-X^2)^{-n} = rescale (-1) (binomialSeries K (-n)), then extract coefficients and use known project lemmas for the convolution. Need Ring.choose negation identity and parity handling.

proof_strategy_notes.failure_analysis: Compile failure: Finset.sum_pow expands to sum over Sym (Fin d) n, not Fin n → Fin d. The generated code incorrectly used congr with funext expecting Sym = function type.
proof_strategy_notes.proof_strategy_note: Use the correct return type of Finset.sum_pow: it expands (∑ a ∈ s, f a)^n = ∑ x ∈ s.sym n, ∏ i ∈ range n, f (x.getI i). Need to relate s.sym n to Fin n → s via a bijection, or use a different expansion lemma.

proof_strategy_notes.failure_analysis: Cannot prove: missing definitions for partition transpose, elementary symmetric function e_n as MvPolynomial, skew Schur function s_{λ/μ}, and the first Jacobi-Trudi theorem. The esymm fallback candidates exist in Mathlib but the exact project-specific wrapper is not visible.
proof_strategy_notes.proof_strategy_note: The visible context has hsymmExt for h_n but no esymmExt for e_n, no transpose operation on NPartition, and no skew Schur definition. These are fundamental missing pieces, not just bridge lemmas.
